data.transformedContent:
<current_datetime>2026-04-25T01:35:33.286Z</current_datetime>

I'm migrating a .NET 10 web API to .NET 11. The app uses EF Core with the Azure Cosmos DB provider.
We have a repository pattern where some methods use synchronous LINQ:

```csharp
public class ProductRepository
{
    private readonly CosmosDbContext _context;

    public List<Product> GetAll()
    {
        return _context.Products.ToList();
    }

    public Product GetById(int id)
    {
        return _context.Products.First(p => p.Id == id);
    }

    public int GetCount()
    {
        return _context.Products.Count();
    }

    public void Save(Product product)
    {
        _context.Products.Add(product);
        _context.SaveChanges();
    }
}
```

In .NET 10 we had the sync-over-async opt-in configured. What breaks in .NET 11?

data.reasoningText:
The user is asking about what breaks when migrating from .NET 10 to .NET 11 with EF Core using the Azure Cosmos DB provider, specifically regarding synchronous LINQ operations.

This is about a known breaking change in EF Core. Let me recall the details:

In EF Core with the Azure Cosmos DB provider, synchronous operations were never natively supported - they were always implemented as sync-over-async. In EF Core 10 (.NET 10), there was an opt-in mechanism to allow this pattern. In .NET 11 (EF Core 11), this sync-over-async support is being removed entirely.


Actually, I need to be more precise here. The Cosmos DB provider has only ever supported async operations natively, and synchronous calls like `ToList()`, `First()`, or `SaveChanges()` were always wrapped as sync-over-async. Starting in EF Core 9, these synchronous operations began throwing exceptions by default, though there was an opt-in flag to re-enable them.

In .NET 11, that opt-in has been completely removed—synchronous LINQ and DbContext operations against Cosmos will throw `InvalidOperationException` with no fallback. So any code using `ToList()`, `First()`, `Count()`, or `SaveChanges()` directly will need to switch to their async equivalents like `ToListAsync()`, `FirstAsync()`, `CountAsync()`, and `SaveChangesAsync()`.
